I need to think about how changing his working hours could affect these metrics. If he starts earlier or works later, maybe he can capture more orders during peak times when there's higher demand. Alternatively, working different hours might help him avoid traffic congestion, which would allow him to deliver more efficiently and possibly earn more per hour.

Considering that his earnings were average, perhaps starting a bit earlier could let him grab some early morning orders before the rush. Maybe from 7:00 instead of 8:00. But does that make sense? Or should he extend his working hours into the evening to catch dinner time deliveries?

Wait, if he starts at 7 AM and ends at 7 PM, that's a longer shift—12 hours. That might be too much, but maybe necessary if there are more opportunities during those times.

Alternatively, maybe he can adjust his start and end times to align with peak delivery periods when riders usually make more money. For example, starting at 8 AM as usual but ending later, say 7 PM, could give him more time to deliver during the dinner rush.

But I should consider if extending hours would lead to more orders or just more fatigue, which might not be efficient. Since his performance was average, perhaps a slight adjustment in working hours could help without overexerting himself.

Wait, another angle: maybe he's working during times when there are too many riders, making it harder to get orders. If he shifts his schedule slightly earlier or later than others, he might have less competition for orders.

So, if most riders start at 8 AM, maybe starting a bit before could give him an edge. Or perhaps ending after 6 PM would allow him to handle more evening deliveries when some riders have already stopped.

But in this case, since his numbers are exactly average, I'm thinking that adjusting his hours slightly might help. Maybe starting earlier and ending later by one hour each. So, from 7 AM to 7 PM.

Alternatively, he could try a different approach—maybe taking shorter shifts but more efficiently. But given that the question is about changing today's working hours based on yesterday's performance, I think adjusting the duration or timing might help improve his metrics.

So, considering all this, I'm leaning towards extending his working hours to capture more peak time orders and possibly increase his earnings and order count.

So putting it all together, I think adjusting my start time slightly later and ending earlier might optimize my performance and earnings. I worked from 8 AM to 7 PM yesterday, which is an 11-hour shift. That's pretty long, and maybe it's taking a toll on my efficiency towards the end of the day. The later hours could be busier times when more riders are online, making it harder to get orders or earn as much per hour.

If I start at 7 AM instead of 8, I might catch the morning surge when people are ordering breakfast or early deliveries. On the flip side, ending earlier—say, at 5 PM—might help me avoid the evening rush when a lot of riders are competing for orders. By stopping earlier, I can rest more and perhaps be fresher the next day. It might also prevent burnout from working too long without a break.

I should also consider my energy levels. Working until 7 PM every day is exhausting. Another thought: maybe the later hours are when food delivery apps have higher demand but also more riders, making it harder to stand out. By adjusting my schedule to be online when there's moderate competition, I could secure more orders without fighting as much with others.

So, balancing all this, starting at 7 AM gives me a head start on the day, and finishing at 5 PM avoids the late rush but still covers enough hours to earn decent money. It might help improve my rankings in both distance and earnings by being more strategic about when I'm available.
